rationale: In the theory section, the citation is used specifically to support the proposed link from immersion to anticipated enjoyment/hedonic value, rather than as a generic background mention. That link is then built into the paper's core mediation logic and tested in the empirical model. Although the citing paper does not extend the rabbit-hole effect itself, it treats Woolley and Sharif (2022) as conceptual prior evidence for a key mechanism step.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Woolley and Sharif (2022) as prior evidence that immersion in one media experience can raise anticipated enjoyment of related subsequent experiences. It uses this idea as a conceptual basis for its own claim that slow-motion-induced immersion increases expected hedonic value and, in turn, luxury perceptions.
